extractions.problem: Predicting and controlling embryonic stem cell differentiation within 3D embryoid bodies is poorly understood, limiting regenerative medicine applications.
extractions.method: Rules-based agent modeling with physics-based aggregation simulation of embryoid bodies, where cells are modeled as incompressible rigid spheres with stochastic binary Oct4 state transitions and intercellular communication rules.
extractions.finding: Loss of Oct4 pluripotency can be modeled as a binary stochastic process with intercellular communication rules, and the emergent spatial patterns are independent of EB structure, size, or cell division.
extractions.dataset: Mouse embryonic stem cells (D3 cell line), embryoid bodies of 250 and 1000 cells
extractions.metric: Radius, circularity, connection count, connection length, Oct4 spatial pattern classification
extractions.baseline: Experimental confocal microscopy Oct4 expression patterns over 7 days
extractions.result: Surface area-to-volume ratio ~8% error vs sphere, simulated aggregate properties match experimental EBs

extractions.problem: Computational models of cellular electroporation are difficult to validate against quantitative molecular uptake data, limiting understanding of membrane permeabilization dynamics.
extractions.method: Two-stage ordinary differential equation model coupling reversible membrane defect formation with irreversible solute transport, fit using Nelder-Mead minimization against fluorescence microscopy time-series data.
extractions.finding: HEPES buffer enables 3- to 5-fold faster molecular transport than PBS or SFDF; interpulse delay up to 100 μs does not appreciably affect uptake in multipulse schemes; per-pulse permeability enhancement decreases with pulse number.
extractions.dataset: Single adherent cells in microfluidic chamber, 3 buffers (PBS, SFDF, HEPES), 4 field strengths (170-400 kV/m), 4 pulse durations (1-1000 μs)
extractions.metric: Sum of squared residuals (SSR), molecular uptake time-series fit
extractions.baseline: Experimental quantitative propidium uptake measurements from Part I
extractions.result: max(SSR_PBS)=0.025, max(SSR_SFDF)=0.0070, max(SSR_HEPES)=0.0066; δ 3-5x larger for HEPES

extractions.problem: As biological network models grow in size and complexity, efficient methods for validating and analyzing their dynamical behavior are lacking.
extractions.method: Model verification (automated unitary testing of logical model specifications) combined with value propagation for analytical computation of environmental/genetic perturbation effects, applied to a Boolean logical model of T cell activation with >200 components and >450 interactions.
extractions.finding: Model verification enables continuous testing during model development; value propagation predicts differential potential of CTLA4 vs PD-1 checkpoint immunotherapies on T cell activation.
extractions.dataset: T cell activation signaling network including CTLA4 and PD-1 checkpoint inhibitory pathways
extractions.metric: Dynamical specification compliance, stable state reachability
extractions.baseline: Manual iterative trial simulations for model validation
extractions.result: Comprehensive model with >200 components, >450 interactions; differential CTLA4 vs PD-1 predictions confirmed by Jupyter notebooks

extractions.problem: Understanding mechanisms of action of endocrine-active chemicals on steroidogenesis requires computational models that can predict steroid concentration responses in vitro.
extractions.method: Mechanistic ODE model of adrenal steroidogenesis incorporating enzymatic reactions and transport pathways, with iterative parameter estimation using MATLAB, validated against time-course LC/MS measurements of 14 steroids in H295R cells.
extractions.finding: The model accurately predicts steroid concentrations under control and metyrapone exposure conditions; metyrapone inhibits CYP11B1 causing cortisol decrease and precursor accumulation in a dose-dependent manner.
extractions.dataset: Human H295R adrenocortical carcinoma cells, 14 steroids measured, metyrapone at 1 and 10 μM
extractions.metric: R-squared for transport equilibrium fit, visual correspondence of time-course predictions
extractions.baseline: Experimental time-course steroid concentrations (control cells, 5 time points)
extractions.result: Transport R² = 0.70-0.99 across 14 steroids; convergence in ~24 min on Intel Core 2 Duo

extractions.problem: Understanding collective cell migration in 3D remains elusive, particularly how microscopic forces and cell-cell/cell-matrix receptor interactions influence group motility and stability.
extractions.method: 3D computational framework modeling cells as overlapping spheres with coarse-grained surface receptor sites for cell-cell adhesion and cell-matrix traction, incorporating ECM viscous resistance and stochastic force components.
extractions.finding: Cell pair stability depends on traction force magnitude and receptor dynamics; mobile surface receptors promote stronger intercellular stability; matrix resistance, cellular stiffness, and adhesion intensity contribute differently to migration with memory effects.
extractions.dataset: Simulated cell pairs and 50-cell clusters with varying receptor dynamics (4 cases of static/dynamic C-C and C-M sites)
extractions.metric: Center-to-center distance, bound vs detached state classification, pair stability over simulation time
extractions.baseline: Four receptor dynamics cases (static/dynamic combinations of cell-cell and cell-matrix sites)
extractions.result: Simulation time scale of days; 50-cell cluster motility demonstrated; memory effects alter pair motility

extractions.problem: The mechanobiological cascade leading to medical device-related pressure ulcers at the cell scale is not well understood, particularly how deformation-inflicted damage and inflammatory edema interact.
extractions.method: Cell-scale finite element modeling of 30 adipocytes near a bony prominence (nasal bridge) using Neo-Hookean hyperelastic material, with a strain-time cell death prediction model and sequential simulation of deformation and edema-induced interstitial pressure.
extractions.finding: Cells adjacent to bony surfaces die first due to adipose-bone stiffness gradients; initial cell death triggers inflammatory edema that escalates further cell damage in a nonlinear, cumulative vicious cycle pattern.
extractions.dataset: 30 mature adipocytes (70 μm diameter) near nasal bone, CPAP ventilation mask scenario
extractions.metric: Effective stress distribution, plasma membrane strain, volumetric PM stress exposure histograms
extractions.baseline: Deformation-only loading vs combined deformation + edema pressure loading
extractions.result: Cell death onset within minutes at high strain; nonlinear escalation demonstrated across 4 simulation steps

extractions.problem: The regulatory mechanisms by which Langerhans cells orchestrate adaptive immune responses in the skin, particularly through interferon regulatory factors, are not established.
extractions.method: Boolean Petri net-based modeling of IRF gene regulatory network, parameterized with transcription factor expression values from whole transcriptome microarray analysis (Affymetrix), with in vitro validation using T cell activation assays.
extractions.finding: IRF1, IRF4, and IRF8 form a key regulatory network in LCs; TNFα and TSLP differentially regulate IRF expression leading to differential CD8 T cell activation; PI3Kγ inhibition shifts LC function from Th2 toward Th1 responses.
extractions.dataset: Primary human Langerhans cells stimulated with TNFα or TSLP, EBV-specific CD8 T cell lines
extractions.metric: IFN-γ ELISpot counts, transcriptome fold change (BETR p<0.05), Pearson correlation (r>0.8)
extractions.baseline: Unstimulated migratory LCs (time 0), DMSO control for PI3Kγ inhibition
extractions.result: 527 differentially regulated genes, 18 clusters identified; in silico predictions confirmed by IFN-γ T cell assays

extractions.problem: The role of periglomerular cells in controlling mitral cell spiking in the olfactory bulb, relative to granule cells, is not fully understood.
extractions.method: Reduced multicompartmental conductance-based model (6 compartments) of the periglomerular cell in NEURON simulator, integrated into a cell triad model with mitral and granule cells to study inhibitory interactions.
extractions.finding: Periglomerular cells are more effective than granule cells at inhibiting mitral cells and are the only ones capable of complete inhibition; granule cells facilitate PG cell inhibition by enlarging the parameter range for complete mitral cell suppression.
extractions.dataset: Computational model of olfactory bulb cell triad (periglomerular, mitral, granule cells)
extractions.metric: Firing rate, spike latency, inhibition completeness, parameter sensitivity (conductance densities)
extractions.baseline: Granule cell inhibition alone vs combined PG + granule cell inhibition
extractions.result: Input resistance 1190 MΩ (matching experimental ~1000 MΩ); stepwise firing rate control demonstrated

extractions.problem: Cell characteristics evolve with increasing passage number in vitro, but the mechanisms driving these changes and their impact on experimental reproducibility are poorly understood.
extractions.method: Random walk lattice-based model simulating in vitro cell culture passaging (passage 0-30) with heterogeneous proliferation rates and passage-induced damage, combined with in silico scratch assays to assess functional consequences.
extractions.finding: Average proliferation rate can either increase or decrease with passaging depending on competition between initial heterogeneity and passage-induced damage; scratch assay closure rates differ significantly between passage numbers, suggesting passage number should always be reported.
extractions.dataset: Simulated near-homogeneous and heterogeneous cell lines, passages 0-30, lattice size 4168×3610
extractions.metric: Mean proliferation rate, standard deviation of proliferation rate, scratch assay closure rate
extractions.baseline: Passage 0 (initial state) vs passage 30; near-homogeneous vs heterogeneous cell lines; no damage vs varying damage levels
extractions.result: Dramatic proliferation rate shift by passage 30 in heterogeneous lines; significantly different scratch closure rates across passage numbers

extractions.problem: Discovering transcription factors for cell reprogramming is largely trial and error; computational models that account for cellular heterogeneity and predict long-term reprogramming outcomes are lacking.
extractions.method: scREMOTE integrates single-cell multiomics (scRNA-seq + scATAC-seq) to compute regulation potential from CRE accessibility, TF motifs, and chromatin conformation, then uses linear regression to iteratively predict long-term TF perturbation effects.
extractions.finding: scREMOTE successfully predicts that Gata3 overexpression reprograms transit-amplifying cells toward inner root sheath and Runx1 toward hair shaft lineages, capturing higher-order gene regulations in hair follicle development.
extractions.dataset: Hair follicle developmental system (simultaneous scRNA-seq and scATAC-seq), 35 highly expressed TFs
extractions.metric: Empirical p-value against random TF-gene pairs, regulation potential enrichment across 5 databases
extractions.baseline: Random subsets of TF-gene pairs (1 million resamples per database); known regulations from TRRUST, hTFtarget, TFBSDB, RegNetwork, MSigDB
extractions.result: All 5 databases significantly enriched (p<0.05 to p<1e-6); successful prediction of Gata3→IRS and Runx1→Hair Shaft reprogramming
